steps.title: Check current knative-eventing deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative-eventing version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
helm list -n knative-eventing 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative-eventing installation.

steps.title: Review knative-eventing configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative-eventing configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get configmap -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative-eventing
```
**Problem**
There is currently no easy way to route events based on some arbitrary conditional logic.
 
This is problem for both, eventing and messaging. For eventing, a route corresponds to a trigger (but filtering is very limited #930).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Conditional Routing
steps.description:
For the "Join" operation, can you use a key-value store like Redis or Memcache for state management, rather than keeping the state in-process?

Other than that, Broker/Trigger is intended to provide the multi-armed bandit solution (assuming that you actually mean "independent selection of events on each subscriber" and not the [Machine Learning technique](https://www.optimizely.com/optimization-glossary/multi-armed-bandit/)); the current implementation is naive, but I'd love to see a proposal

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get events -n knative-eventing --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Conditional Routing" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative-eventing installed or the issue environment reproducible.
